2603.15401.title: SWE-Skills-Bench: Do Agent Skills Actually Help in Real-World Software Engineering?
2603.15401.paper_summary: Agent skills, structured procedural knowledge packages injected at inference time, are increasingly used to augment LLM agents on software engineering tasks. However, their real utility in end-to-end development settings remains unclear. We present SWE-Skills-Bench, the first requirement-driven benchmark that isolates the marginal utility of agent skills in real-world software engineering (SWE). It pairs 49 public SWE skills with authentic GitHub repositories pinned at fixed commits and requirement documents with explicit acceptance criteria, yielding approximately 565 task instances across six SWE subdomains. We introduce a deterministic verification framework that maps each task's acceptance criteria to execution-based tests, enabling controlled paired evaluation with and without the skill. Our results show that skill injection benefits are far more limited than rapid adoption suggests: 39 of 49 skills yield zero pass-rate improvement, and the average gain is only +1.2%. Token overhead varies from modest savings to a 451% increase while pass rates remain unchanged. Only seven specialized skills produce meaningful gains (up to +30%), while three degrade performance (up to -10%) due to version-mismatched guidance conflicting with project context. These findings suggest that agent skills are a narrow intervention whose utility depends strongly on domain fit, abstraction level, and contextual compatibility. SWE-Skills-Bench provides a testbed for evaluating the design, selection, and deployment of skills in software engineering agents. SWE-Skills-Bench is available at https://github.com/GeniusHTX/SWE-Skills-Bench.
2603.15401.paper_summary_zh: 代理技能，結構化的程序知識包在推理時注入，越來越多地用於增強LLM代理在軟體工程任務中的表現。<lb/>然而，它們在端到端開發環境中的實際效用仍然不明朗。<lb/>我們提出了SWE-Skills-Bench，這是第一個需求驅動的基準，旨在孤立代理技能在現實世界軟體工程（SWE）中的邊際效用。<lb/>它將49個公共SWE技能與固定提交的真實GitHub倉庫和具有明確接受標準的需求文檔配對，產生了約565個任務實例，涵蓋六個SWE子領域。<lb/>我們引入了一個確定性的驗證框架，將每個任務的接受標準映射到基於執行的測試，實現了有技能和無技能的受控配對評估。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，技能注入的好處遠比快速採用所暗示的要有限：49個技能中有39個的通過率沒有改善，平均增益僅為+1.2%。<lb/>令牌開銷從適度的節省到451%的增加不等，而通過率保持不變。<lb/>只有七個專業技能產生了有意義的增益（最高可達+30%），而三個因版本不匹配的指導與項目上下文衝突而降低了性能（最高可達-10%）。<lb/>這些發現表明，代理技能是一種狹隘的干預，其效用強烈依賴於領域適配性、抽象水平和上下文兼容性。<lb/>SWE-Skills-Bench提供了一個測試平台，用於評估技能在軟體工程代理中的設計、選擇和部署。<lb/>SWE-Skills-Bench可在https://github.com/GeniusHTX/SWE-Skills-Bench獲得。
2603.15401.author: Tingxu Han et.al.
2603.15401.authors: Tingxu Han, Yi Zhang, Wei Song, Chunrong Fang, Zhenyu Chen, Youcheng Sun, Lijie Hu
